channel.title: easyZip Updates
channel.description: Updates for easyZip — a modern macOS archive browser.

item.title: Version 0.1.1
item.description:
Bug fixes

  • Fix: clicking on an entry in the archive browser now reliably selects it (previously the row-level drag handler intercepted mouse-down, so single clicks were silently swallowed and Quick Look via Space wouldn't trigger).
  • Drag-out migrated from the legacy .onDrag API to the modern Transferable / .draggable API, which coexists cleanly with list selection — drag only begins after a small movement, leaving clicks free for selection.
  • Selection is now cleared when a new archive is opened, so stale IDs from the previous archive don't shadow fresh rows.

item.title: Version 0.1.0
item.description:
First public release

  • Open ~20 archive formats (zip, 7z, tar.*, rar (read), gzip, bz2, xz, zstd, cpio, iso, ar, xar, lzh, deb, rpm, …)
  • Browse contents in a Liquid-Glass-styled split view
  • Extract everything or just the entries you select
  • Drag entries out to the Finder; preview with Quick Look (Space)
  • Create new archives (ZIP incl. AES-256, 7z, tar.*, gzip, bz2, xz, zstd)
  • Search inside an archive, diff two archives, test integrity
  • Shortcuts intents: Extract Archive, Compress Files, Test Archive
